--- title: "Foundations of Data Analysis for Operations Research" --- ## Why Data Analysis Matters in Operations Research Operations Research (OR) is all about making informed decisions to optimize processes and systems. Data analysis is the backbone of OR, providing the insights needed to model, analyze, and solve complex problems. Whether it's optimizing supply chains, scheduling, or resource allocation, understanding the data is crucial for effective decision-making. Before optimizing, we must understand and prepare our data. ::: {#fe421ebf .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import seaborn as sns # Sample dataset for demonstration data = { 'product': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D'], 'demand': [150, 200, 120, 180], 'cost': [12.5, 15.0, 10.8, 14.2], 'lead_time': [5, 3, 7, 4], 'capacity': [300, 400, 250, 350] } df = pd.DataFrame(data) print("Shape:", df.shape) display(df.head()) display(df.describe()) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Shape: (4, 5) ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
product demand cost lead_time capacity
0 A 150 12.5 5 300
1 B 200 15.0 3 400
2 C 120 10.8 7 250
3 D 180 14.2 4 350
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
demand cost lead_time capacity
count 4.0 4.000000 4.000000 4.000000
mean 162.5 13.125000 4.750000 325.000000
std 35.0 1.867931 1.707825 64.549722
min 120.0 10.800000 3.000000 250.000000
25% 142.5 12.075000 3.750000 287.500000
50% 165.0 13.350000 4.500000 325.000000
75% 185.0 14.400000 5.500000 362.500000
max 200.0 15.000000 7.000000 400.000000
``` ::: ::: ## Data Cleaning and Preprocessing Data often comes with issues like missing values, outliers, or inconsistent formats. Cleaning and preprocessing are crucial steps to ensure the quality of our analysis. ::: {#50a7ec1a .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Check for missing values print("Missing values:\n", df.isnull().sum()) # Handle outliers using IQR method Q1 = df['demand'].quantile(0.25) Q3 = df['demand'].quantile(0.75) IQR = Q3 - Q1 outliers = df[(df['demand'] < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR)) | (df['demand'] > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))] print("Outliers:\n", outliers) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Missing values: product 0 demand 0 cost 0 lead_time 0 capacity 0 dtype: int64 Outliers: Empty DataFrame Columns: [product, demand, cost, lead_time, capacity] Index: [] ``` ::: ::: ## Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) EDA helps us uncover patterns, relationships, and insights in our data. Visualizations and summary statistics are powerful tools for this purpose. ::: {#9786ef25 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Correlation heatmap - only numeric columns numeric_df = df.select_dtypes(include=['number']) # or use numeric_only=True plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6)) sns.heatmap(numeric_df.corr(), annot=True, cmap='coolwarm', linewidths=0.5, fmt='.2f') plt.title('Correlation Heatmap (Numeric Features Only)') plt.show() # Pairplot - also safer (or limit to numeric columns) sns.pairplot(numeric_df) plt.suptitle('Pairplot of Numeric Features', y=1.02) plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](01-foundations_files/figure-html/cell-4-output-1.png){width=620 height=505} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](01-foundations_files/figure-html/cell-4-output-2.png){width=946 height=982} ::: ::: ## Conclusion Data analysis is an essential foundation for operations research. By understanding and preparing our data, we can build more accurate models and make better decisions. In the next chapters, we will delve deeper
